opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I read my test results and it says my thyroid is failing. I feel incredibly weak, I can't catch my breath, and my heart is skipping beats. I'm terrified my body is shutting down.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I read the results an hour ago and I'm panicking that my body is shutting down.

symptom_narrative.onset: about an hour ago after reading results
symptom_narrative.character: racing heart, fast breathing, weakness, a sense of 'skipped beats'
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: palpitations feel regular; no chest pain, no fainting; hyperventilating; no leg swelling/calf pain; no fever

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: subclinical an autoimmune thyroid condition (raised TSH, normal T4, thyroid antibodies)

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: wants to be told her body isn't shutting down

communication_style.typical_message_style: rapid, fearful, cites what she read

objective_data_offered.value: my watch shows about 100 and it feels regular
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: wearable during acute anxiety; not a diagnostic ECG
